title: Break down sync_scene() in lib.rs
description:
sync_scene() is 116 lines (lib.rs:554-669) handling multiple responsibilities:
- Sync room objects to scene graph
- Read avatar position/yaw from controller
- Compute avatar offset
- Lerp avatar yaw
- Dead reckoning + position smoothing for remote users
- Sync user avatars to scene

Extract standalone functions:
- fn update_remote_user_positions(users: &mut [RoomUser], dt: f32, now: f64)
- fn compute_avatar_transform(user: &RoomUser, avatar_y_offset: f32, smooth_yaw: f32) -> Transform

Complexity: high
close_reason: Broke down sync_scene() into sync_objects_to_scene, lerp_yaw, update_remote_user_positions, sync_users_to_scene

title: Extract session-detail-application pattern in lib.rs
description:
The same session detail application logic is repeated 3 times in lib.rs:

1. restore_sessions_from_ndb() lines 1613-1650
2. poll_session_state_events() lines 1806-1867
3. poll_remote_conversation_events() (inline)

Each applies hostname, custom_title, home_dir, sets up subscriptions, and seeds live threading data.

## Related duplications
- Live conversation subscription setup: repeated 4x (1630-1650, 1846-1866, 828-849, permission subs)
- Remote session detection (hostname mismatch): duplicated at 1589-1596 and 1820-1827
- Note polling boilerplate (poll->txn->iterate->get_note): repeated 3x at 1318-1327, 1670-1693, 1910-1925
- Session state timestamp comparison: duplicated at 1614-1616 and 1750-1751

## Approach
Extract standalone functions:
- apply_loaded_session_config()
- setup_conversation_subscription()
- is_session_remote(state, hostname)
- Note-polling iterator helper

title: H4: stop() doesnt clear self.service
description: manager.rs:138-143 service field stays Some after stop(). send() tries dead channel.
close_reason: Fixed: stop() sets self.service=None

build_space() is 95 lines (convert.rs:111-205). The per-object loop body (lines 148-197) builds a Cell with attributes for each RoomObject.

Extract:

This would reduce build_space() to ~40 lines of orchestration.

Complexity: medium
close_reason: Extracted build_object_cell() and object_type_to_cell() from build_space() in convert.rs

description: GitHub #575: Contact lists don't refresh periodically as they should. See https://github.com/damus-io/notedeck/issues/575

title: Extract shared backend logic into backend/shared.rs
description:
messages_to_prompt() and get_pending_user_messages() are identical in claude.rs and codex.rs. Permission request handling (~70 lines) is nearly identical across both. Session command spawning, subagent creation/completion, and tool result summary generation are also duplicated.

## Files
- crates/notedeck_dave/src/backend/claude.rs
- crates/notedeck_dave/src/backend/codex.rs

## Specific duplications
- messages_to_prompt(): claude.rs:76-114, codex.rs:1152-1178
- get_pending_user_messages(): claude.rs:119-131, codex.rs:1181-1193
- Permission request handling: claude.rs:335-418, codex.rs:715-761
- Session command spawning: claude.rs:707-718, codex.rs:1260-1271
- Subagent creation: claude.rs:430-455, codex.rs:499-521
- Tool result summary: claude.rs:547-551, codex.rs:673-682,796-805,828-843

## Approach
Create a new backend/shared.rs module with standalone functions. Update claude.rs and codex.rs to call into the shared module.
notes:
Extracted messages_to_prompt(), get_pending_user_messages(), prepare_prompt(), SessionCommand, and SessionHandle into backend/shared.rs. Both claude.rs and codex.rs now import from the shared module. All 219 tests pass. Remaining duplication (permission handling, subagent utils, tool result summary) can be done in a follow-up.
Second commit: extracted send_tool_result() and complete_subagent() to shared.rs. Eliminated 4 repeated tool result construction sites and 2 subagent completion sites. Remaining: permission request handling duplication.
close_reason: Extracted 5 shared utilities (messages_to_prompt, get_pending_user_messages, prepare_prompt, SessionCommand/SessionHandle, send_tool_result, complete_subagent, should_auto_accept, forward_permission_to_ui) into backend/shared.rs across 3 commits. Net ~100 lines removed from claude.rs/codex.rs.

title: Unify RoomSubscription and PresenceSubscription
description:
RoomSubscription and PresenceSubscription in subscriptions.rs have identical new() and poll() implementations differing only by the kind constant.

Extract a shared helper or generic:
- fn create_kind_subscription(ndb: &Ndb, kind: u64) -> Subscription
- fn poll_subscription(sub: &Subscription, ndb: &Ndb, txn: &Transaction) -> Vec<Note>

Or use a single generic NoteSubscription<const KIND: u16> struct.

File: subscriptions.rs lines 18-76

Complexity: low
close_reason: Implemented in commit f84c97686e50

title: H2: Account switch doesnt update desktop notification pubkey

comments.author: e
comments.text: Re-assessed: desktop is NOT affected (process_relay_message rebuilds from live accounts cache). Only Android is affected — foreground service subscribes with single pubkey from SharedPreferences.

title: S2: Signing keypair in static memory never zeroed

title: Ctrl+P not dropping from NeedsInput to Done
description: Commit c6a96d8dbfef is supposed to enable dropping from NeedsInput to Done with Ctrl+P, but it's not working. The commit message says Ctrl+P should navigate backward within a priority group and drop to the next lower priority level when at the first item (NeedsInput → Error → Done).
close_reason: Fixed swapped keybindings in keybindings.rs:82-90 - Ctrl+N now returns FocusQueueNext (higher priority) and Ctrl+P returns FocusQueuePrev (lower priority)

title: Consolidate session module duplications
description:
Several duplications exist across the session_*.rs files.

## SessionState construction (verbatim copy)
- session_loader.rs:292-304 (load_session_states)
- session_loader.rs:338-350 (latest_valid_session)
Extract: build_session_state_from_note(note, session_id) -> SessionState

## Truncation functions (identical implementations)
- session_loader.rs:353-359 truncate()
- session_jsonl.rs:306-313 truncate_str()
- session_discovery.rs:62-68 and 74-79 (inline)
Consolidate into a single shared utility.

## Builder tag patterns in session_events.rs
- build_permission_request_event() lines 655-671
- build_permission_response_event() lines 702-721
- build_session_state_event() lines 760-765
All share identical source/t-tag addition logic.
Extract: add_common_event_tags(), add_permission_base_tags()

## Large functions to break up
- build_events() (130 lines, 221-350)
- build_single_event() (94 lines, 419-512)
- load_session_messages() (153 lines, 92-244)

title: Break up Dave::update() and Dave::process_events()
description:
Dave::update() (~412 lines, lib.rs:2456-2868) and Dave::process_events() (~387 lines, lib.rs:594-981) are the two largest methods in the codebase. Each contains multiple distinct phases that could be standalone functions.

## update() extractions
- process_negentropy_sync() (~75 lines, 2463-2529)
- handle_archive_conversion() (~80 lines, 2631-2711)
- poll_message_load() (~30 lines, 2713-2742)
- process_session_states() (~45 lines, 2799-2820)
- publish_relay_events() (~25 lines, 2777-2791)

## process_events() extractions
- process_session_tokens() (~65 lines, 609-681)
- handle_tool_call_execution() (~80 lines, 687-716)
- handle_permission_request_event() (~45 lines, 719-776)
- handle_stream_completion() (~55 lines, 923-969)

## Also large in lib.rs
- poll_remote_conversation_events() (~219 lines, 1886-2105)
- poll_session_state_events() (~212 lines, 1665-1877)
- restore_sessions_from_ndb() (~113 lines, 1546-1659)
- poll_remote_permission_responses() (~103 lines, 1300-1403)

## Approach
Extract into standalone functions that take explicit parameters rather than &mut self where possible, improving testability and making data flow explicit.

title: Break down show_room_view() in room_view.rs
description:
show_room_view() is 340 lines (room_view.rs:173-512) — the largest function in the crate. It mixes input handling, camera control, and rendering.

Extract standalone functions:
- fn handle_drag_input(ui, response, rect, state, r) -> Option<NostrverseAction>
- fn handle_click_input(response, rect, state, r) -> Option<NostrverseAction>
- fn handle_keyboard_input(ui, state) -> Option<NostrverseAction>
- fn handle_camera_input(ui, response, r)

Complexity: high
close_reason: Extracted handle_drag_start, compute_initial_drag, apply_drag_update, handle_keyboard_input from show_room_view
